title: Weather station pcp04 has 18 days of missing precipitation
explanation: A gap of this length during a known wet-season month will bias the basin water balance. SWAT+ fills -99 days with the long-term mean, which flattens storm events and under-predicts runoff peaks downstream.
suggestion: Infill from the nearest neighbour station (pcp02, 12 km away) using inverse-distance weighting, or trim the simulation window to skip 2014.

title: Channel cha019 has no downstream receiver
evidence: dsobj_typ = 'null', dsobj = 0 for cha019 but cha019 is not flagged as outfall in object.cnt
explanation: A non-outfall channel with a null downstream object will silently drop all water routed to it. The outfall detector found a single outlet at cha033; cha019 should route into it.
suggestion: In QSWAT+, re-run stream delineation or manually set cha019.dsobj → cha033 in chandeg.con.

title: ET/precip ratio of 0.83 is at the upper range
explanation: For humid subtropical basins, the literature typical range is 0.55–0.85 (White et al. 2014). A ratio at the edge of the range suggests ET may be slightly over-predicted — commonly traced to low ESCO or aggressive PET (Penman-Monteith with undercalibrated canopy resistance).
suggestion: Inspect ESCO (currently 0.95) and consider lowering to 0.75 for forested HRUs. Compare against a PET-only sensitivity run.

title: Plant operation scheduled in month with T_avg < 5°C
evidence: pcp05 Dec 2015 T_avg = 3.4°C · plant op date = 2015-12-18
explanation: SWAT+ will accept the schedule but PHU accumulation will stall; the crop will not reach harvest before the next growing season, which typically manifests as stuck LAI and zero biomass at harvest.
suggestion: Use auto-plant with heat-unit trigger (PHU = 0.15) instead of a fixed date, or shift to March.

title: 27 HRUs have slope > 45%
explanation: Steep HRUs produce unrealistically high sediment yields under the default MUSLE formulation. This is not a setup error but will dominate the sediment signal at the outfall.
suggestion: Consider a cons_practice.lum lookup with terracing or contour tillage for the affected HRUs.

title: ALPHA_BF hit upper bound in iteration 18
explanation: Parameters pinned to a bound mean either the bound is wrong or another parameter is compensating. ALPHA_BF = 1 implies near-instantaneous baseflow recession, which is unphysical for this basin.
suggestion: Widen ALPHA_BF to [0.01, 0.8] and add GW_DELAY to the parameter set; they compensate each other.

title: Baseflow index of 0.71 is high for this land use mix
explanation: A BFI of 0.71 would be typical for a deeply permeable sandy basin. URU's dominant soil is clay-loam, which typically yields BFI ≈ 0.4–0.55.
suggestion: Lower CN2 by 5–8% and raise SURLAG; also verify the soils.sol layer 1 K_sat is not orders-of-magnitude off.

title: Warmup period (3 years) meets Daggupati 2015 guidance
explanation: Warmup length is adequate for a mid-sized basin with shallow aquifer storage.

title: Basin outfall auto-detected: cha033
evidence: cha033 is the only channel with no downstream receiver
explanation: Modules 2 and 3 will default their evaluation channel to cha033. Change with `--channel` if a nested gauge is the target.

title: ESCO has near-zero sensitivity rank
explanation: Low sensitivity means either the parameter genuinely does not matter at this basin scale, or the sensitivity probe used a range too narrow to exercise it.
suggestion: Drop ESCO from calibration to free token budget; keep fixed at 0.80.

title: 12-year simulation period is adequate for climate variability
explanation: Covers at least one ENSO cycle; daily metrics will be less noisy than in shorter runs.

title: 2 management operations reference unknown fertilizer
explanation: SWAT+ will fall back to the first fertilizer entry silently; nutrient loads downstream will be wrong but the model will run.
suggestion: Add urea_32 to fertilizer.frt (check the SWAT+ editor's default DB) or remap rows 112 & 118.

title: 8 distinct soil types across 312 HRUs
explanation: Reasonable diversity — avoid over-simplifying. Uniqueness at 8 soil types is typical for a basin this size.

title: PET method is Hargreaves but solar radiation is available
evidence: pet = 2 (Hargreaves) · slr.cli has 5 stations with full coverage
explanation: Penman-Monteith (pet = 1) is physically preferred when all climate inputs are available; Hargreaves is a fallback for data-sparse regions.
suggestion: Switch codes.bsn:pet to 1 and re-run. Compare basin ET against the current run.

title: Decision tables parsed cleanly
evidence: lum.dtl: 12 tables · res_rel.dtl: 3 tables · no header mismatches
explanation: Decision tables are often silently corrupted by hand-editing; yours parse cleanly.
